title: Integral
short: The reverse of differentiation; the indefinite integral accumulates a function, ∫f(x) dx.
full:
Integration is the inverse of differentiation. The indefinite integral ∫f(x) dx, also called the antiderivative, is the function whose derivative is f. For f(x) = 2x, the integral is x² (+ C).

Because the derivative of a constant is zero, every indefinite integral carries an arbitrary constant of integration C — the CAS tab omits it for brevity. A definite integral, over an interval, gives the signed area under the curve.

The key rule is the power rule in reverse: ∫xⁿ dx = xⁿ⁺¹/(n+1) for n ≠ −1.
